identity.title: Prove or reject the pure-Rust fingerprint-capable transport path

authorization.reference: Operator authorized creation of a detailed no-context STUB for this future work. This authorization does not permit product implementation while lifecycle.status is STUB and does not approve BoringSSL, curl-impersonate, curl_cffi, or another native/runtime dependency.

blockers: STUB status forbids implementation until the Operator promotes and activates this packet.
WP-FF-003-executable-gate-bootstrap-v1 must be VALIDATED before a prototype package is added.

WP-FF-004 must supply mandatory transport/fingerprint corpus cases and sanitized transcript rules, and WP-FF-005 must supply request/cancellation/error contracts or explicitly activated co-development scope.
No BoringSSL-backed transport, curl-impersonate/curl_cffi bridge, external downloader, native TLS dependency, or other runtime/native exception is authorized.
lifecycle.next_action: After prerequisites and explicit activation, research current pure-Rust transport/TLS/fingerprint capabilities and limitations, freeze mandatory cases and security/resource thresholds, then promote to READY_FOR_DEV without adding an unapproved dependency.

scope.summary: Build a prototype-only pure-Rust transport capability spike and determine with executable corpus evidence whether mandatory fingerprint, streaming, redirect, cookie, SSRF, replay, pooling, and cancellation requirements can be met inside the authorized dependency boundary.
in_scope: Current primary-source evaluation of maintained pure-Rust HTTP/TLS transport candidates, features, wire controls, security posture, maintenance, and transitive dependency boundaries.
A non-shipped adapter implementing the logical streaming transport capability contract without extractor selection of a concrete backend.

Mandatory fingerprint/wire corpus plus deterministic local cases for HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2, proxies, ranges, streaming, compression, redirect stripping, cookies, DNS provenance, SSRF/rebinding, pooling identity, replay, and cancellation.
Structured capability negotiation and diagnostics showing requested, satisfied, and blocked capabilities with no silent downgrade.
Sanitized transcripts with request IDs, timing phases, selected address/proxy/ALPN/protocol/wire identity, pool-key identity, and secret normalization.
Bounded metadata/body/decompression/redirect/retry behavior, byte-credit admission before body acceptance, cancellation acknowledgement, and defined pool reuse/discard outcome.
Machine-readable PASS_PURE_RUST_PATH or FAILED_SPIKE_REQUIRES_OPERATOR_DECISION evidence without a parity claim.
non_goals: Broad network stack or extractor implementation.
Adding an unapproved native/runtime dependency or arbitrary external downloader.
Claiming browser fingerprint compatibility from ordinary HTTP success.
Silently downgrading a required fingerprint, proxy, DNS/SSRF, streaming, or cancellation capability.
Claiming full replacement parity if any mandatory corpus case requires an unavailable capability.
dependencies: WP-FF-004 mandatory transport/fingerprint cases and sanitized transcript normalization
WP-FF-005 request, capability, identity, event/error, and cancellation contracts
Explicit Operator approval before any dependency outside Rust plus FFmpeg/ffprobe

acceptance_matrix.requirement: Current research compares pure-Rust candidates using official source, releases, issues, security evidence, features, wire-control implementation, tests, and dependency graphs; the selected minimal candidate and rejected options are recorded without authorizing an exception.

Every mandatory deterministic transport/fingerprint corpus case emits requested/satisfied/blocked capabilities, connection and wire identity, stable case ID, normalized transcript, result/error, and exact mismatch; all mandatory cases pass or the verdict requires Operator decision.

Redirect, cookie/credential, proxy, DNS/SSRF/rebinding, pool partition, retry, range, decompression, metadata/body bounds, streaming byte credits, cancellation acknowledgement, and pool reuse/discard invariants pass positive, boundary, and negative probes.

No required capability silently downgrades; unavailable address-level SSRF evidence in proxy mode and unavailable fingerprint/wire behavior produce typed blocked-capability outcomes before unsafe execution.

Cargo graph, source/build-script, clean-artifact, and linkage/provenance checks prove no BoringSSL, curl/curl-impersonate/curl_cffi, arbitrary external downloader, unapproved FFI/native link, build download, or prebuilt binary was introduced.

Canonical architecture, PR, and triggered deep gates pass and the aggregate machine-readable verdict is PASS_PURE_RUST_PATH or FAILED_SPIKE_REQUIRES_OPERATOR_DECISION; neither verdict claims full network or replacement parity.

The no-context model manual documents deterministic/local and opt-in live cases, report fields, transcript sanitization, security boundaries, failure reproduction, cancellation recovery, and the dependency-exception boundary.

blockers: Planning-only STUB; no implementation checkpoint exists.
last_checkpoint.next_action: Wait for prerequisites and explicit activation, then perform source-level transport research and freeze the capability/security corpus.

not_proven: SSRF, redirect, cookie, pooling, streaming, replay, or cancellation safety
Dependency-boundary compliance of a future implementation
Full network compatibility or replacement parity

stub_implementation_brief.base_scope: Resolve the pure-Rust fingerprint-capable transport feasibility question with a backend-neutral non-shipped adapter and a deterministic security/compatibility corpus.

implementation_readiness_gate_items: Approved Rust transport fingerprint, redirect, cookie, SSRF, replay, streaming, pooling, and cancellation result
Executable phase gates with machine-readable reports

promotion_requirements: Research current pure-Rust HTTP/TLS candidates through official source, docs, releases, issues, security advisories, tests, benchmarks, dependency graphs, and wire/fingerprint implementations.
Inspect WP-004 cases/transcript normalization and WP-005 request/capability/cancellation contracts; reuse them rather than inventing parallel schemas.
Freeze mandatory fingerprints and protocol/security cases, proxy trust assumptions, DNS evidence requirements, byte/resource ceilings, timing topology, and failed-spike verdict before implementation.
Any measured need for a native/runtime exception must stop at operator-ready evidence and a separate explicit approval request.
planned_outputs: Current pure-Rust transport candidate evidence matrix
Sanitized replay transcript and capability report
Boundary, negative, cancellation, and pooling tests
Operator-ready failed-spike evidence when mandatory capability is unavailable

high_roi_additions.addition: Build one transport conformance harness against the logical capability port.
high_roi_additions.why_high_roi: Every candidate must satisfy the same cases; backend-independent tests make replacement and future adapters cheap.
high_roi_additions.gap_or_risk_closed: Prevents extractor-specific network behavior and backend lock-in.
high_roi_additions.reuse: WP-005 transport contracts, WP-004 corpus IDs, and local protocol server.
high_roi_additions.verification: A deliberately deficient backend reports blocked capabilities while the candidate runs the identical suite.

high_roi_additions.addition: Add explicit pool-key and credential-partition introspection to diagnostic reports.
high_roi_additions.why_high_roi: Pool identity is already computed and is central to security; exposing normalized components makes hidden cross-session reuse testable.
high_roi_additions.gap_or_risk_closed: Prevents cookie, credential, proxy, TLS, fingerprint, and connection-bound authentication leakage.
high_roi_additions.reuse: Structured diagnostics, session partition IDs, and sanitized transcript normalization.
high_roi_additions.verification: Vary each behavior-affecting input independently and prove incompatible contexts never share a pool.

high_roi_additions.addition: Make SSRF/DNS-rebinding fixtures reusable by all later URL and redirect work.
high_roi_additions.why_high_roi: The local protocol server and address policy are already in scope; reusable cases avoid repeating a critical security harness.
high_roi_additions.gap_or_risk_closed: Covers submitted, redirected, nested, multi-address, rebinding, proxy, IPv4/IPv6, and special-use destination failures.
high_roi_additions.reuse: fforager-testkit local server, stable case IDs, and request diagnostics.
high_roi_additions.verification: Mutation fixtures bypassing validation at any hop or substituting an unapproved resolved address must fail.

high_roi_additions.addition: Emit deterministic failure bundles and separate cold/warm/pool-reuse timing topologies.
high_roi_additions.why_high_roi: Report metadata and replay transcripts already exist, enabling reproducible cross-agent diagnosis and honest later performance work.
high_roi_additions.gap_or_risk_closed: Prevents chat-only failures and misleading speed claims from unequal connection/cache state.
high_roi_additions.reuse: WP-003 reports, WP-004 normalization, request IDs, timing phases, and benchmark manifest conventions.
high_roi_additions.verification: Reported local failures replay identically; performance reports identify topology and follow correctness gates.

existing_systems_to_reuse: WP-003 architecture/dependency policy, xtask reports, and negative fixtures
WP-004 mandatory cases, stable IDs, transcript normalization, and deterministic/live partition
Design local protocol test server and fforager-testkit
Design Section 13 logical transport contract and structured diagnostics
gaps_closed: Fingerprint-capable pure-Rust feasibility becomes measured rather than assumed.
Transport capability downgrade and selection become explicit and observable.
Redirect, credential, SSRF, pool, streaming, replay, and cancellation invariants gain reusable executable proof.
A failed candidate yields an Operator dependency-decision surface without silently violating the boundary.

risk_scenarios.risk: Ordinary HTTP success is mistaken for fingerprint compatibility.
risk_scenarios.failure_scenario: Requests succeed against generic servers while mandatory wire/TLS/HTTP2 fingerprints fail on target cases.
risk_scenarios.mitigation_and_hardening: Require explicit requested/satisfied fingerprint and wire identities and gate selection on mandatory fingerprint corpus outcomes.
risk_scenarios.verification_needed: Known-sensitive deterministic or controlled cases distinguish required profiles and fail on wrong/absent wire identity.

risk_scenarios.risk: Pool reuse crosses credential or fingerprint boundaries.
risk_scenarios.failure_scenario: A connection created for one cookie, proxy, TLS, client-certificate, or fingerprint context serves another.
risk_scenarios.mitigation_and_hardening: Include every behavior/security-affecting input in immutable pool keys and expose normalized key identity for tests.
risk_scenarios.verification_needed: Pairwise and generated partition tests show incompatible contexts never reuse a connection; compatible contexts may reuse only as declared.

risk_scenarios.risk: Redirect, DNS rebinding, or proxy behavior bypasses SSRF policy.
risk_scenarios.failure_scenario: A permitted URL resolves or redirects to loopback/private/special-use space, or proxy mode hides destination evidence and proceeds.
risk_scenarios.mitigation_and_hardening: Revalidate every submitted/redirected/nested URL, validate all resolved addresses, bind to one approved result, and reject operations requiring unavailable proxy destination evidence.
risk_scenarios.verification_needed: IPv4/IPv6 special-use, mixed-result, rebinding, redirect-chain, nested-resource, and untrusted-proxy cases fail closed.

risk_scenarios.risk: Streaming, decompression, retries, or cancellation become unbounded.
risk_scenarios.failure_scenario: A slow/expanding/retrying response consumes unbounded bytes or leaves a poisoned reusable connection after cancellation.
risk_scenarios.mitigation_and_hardening: Require byte credits before acceptance, metadata/body/decompression/redirect/retry ceilings, deadlines, cancellation acknowledgement, and explicit pool discard/reuse state.
risk_scenarios.verification_needed: Slow, infinite, zip-bomb, reset, retry-storm, one-over-limit, cancel-race, and stalled-consumer probes remain bounded.

risk_scenarios.risk: Candidate transitively introduces forbidden native or build artifacts.
risk_scenarios.failure_scenario: Default features, build scripts, FFI, Cargo links, downloads, or prebuilt binaries violate the approved boundary.
risk_scenarios.mitigation_and_hardening: Inspect features/source/build scripts/transitive graph and clean artifacts, disable defaults, pin lockfile, and fail architecture policy on undeclared mechanisms.
risk_scenarios.verification_needed: Cargo metadata, source/build scans, lockfile, clean-artifact smoke, and linkage/provenance inspection pass with prohibition mutation fixtures.

verification_needs: Mandatory fingerprint/wire corpus and deterministic local protocol suite
Streaming byte-credit, body/decompression/metadata/retry bounds, stalled consumer, timeout, and cancellation probes
Sanitization/secret canaries and deterministic replay bundles
no_context_execution_order: Read startup authority, active packet, topology, build rules, and cited design/readiness anchors.
Confirm promotion from STUB, prerequisite proof, and exact dependency authorization boundary.
Complete current source-level research and freeze capability/security/corpus/verdict manifests.
Implement the backend-neutral port, local harness, and minimal non-shipped pure-Rust candidate adapter.
Run capability correctness and security first, then bounds/cancellation/fault tests, then topology-separated performance measurements.
Emit PASS_PURE_RUST_PATH or FAILED_SPIKE_REQUIRES_OPERATOR_DECISION with exact residual uncertainty; never silently add an exception.
Update manual and packet proof; merge only after canonical gates and independent adversarial validation pass.
